- g15 `a55c3afa` fitness 7.773 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-stiffness pivot away from the elite family's thin/minimum-mass arms (wide arm_width=1.4, no waist narrowing, thick arm_thickness=0.007, carbon) that failed only on rotor/deck clearance -- fixed by raising deck_gap from the failed 0.027 to 0.04, well clear of the stack.

SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 6 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate a072202c — Every elite sits at the arm_waist_scale floor (0.55, maximum mid-shaft narrowing) paired with minimum width -- this pivot decouples the two, isolating waist taper as its own stiffness lever by raising arm_waist_scale to 1.10 (near-constant arm width along the shaft, no pinch point to flex/resonate under storm gusts) while keeping arm_width_scale, arm_thickness, sweep and plate geometry at the elitflew at 6.630 Wh/km
  • candidate cadb6dac — Prior material pivots away from carbon always failed validity (arm tip deflection / plate-web-too-thin) because they kept the elite's thin-waist thin-arm shape unchanged for a lower-modulus polymer -- this pivot properly compensates by isolating PETG (damped, tough) with arm_thickness raised to 0.0065 and arm_waist_scale relaxed to 0.80 to prevent tip deflection, and plate_thickness_scale set to 1invalid — arm tip deflection (petg)
  • candidate f2511166 — Replaces the failed 14-deg rear-sweep pivot: trims the deviation to 10 deg past stock and grows plate_length/width_scale to 1.22/1.25 (above the prior 1.15/1.2 that let the tongue bolts miss the main plate) while keeping the same lean thin-arm carbon build to isolate wide-stance stiffness from mass penalties.flew at 6.641 Wh/km
  • candidate e1d629ce — rejected pre-flight (arm tongue bolts miss the main plate) — The elite family has converged on a symmetric near-stock, minimum-everything (thin waist, thin width, floor thickness) stance that has plateaued on storm/crosswind; this pivot keeps the elite's low-mass thin arms but pushes rear_sweep_deg alone to a genuinely wide 50 deg (asymmetric DeadCat stance) with plate scales grown per the sweep-deviation rule, testing whether real aerodynamic/yaw-stability

inputs
⟳ pivot generation — bred with far parents after a plateau✦ claude designer · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · 3 design(s) injected, 2 rejected pre-flight
elites carried over unchanged2
claude-designed3
crossovers (tournament parents)4
pivot crosses (far parent)7
mutation σ 0.114 of gene range
pivot σ 0.300 on 50% of non-elite slots
crossover 90% · immigrants 10%
tournament k=3 · elitism 2 · patience 3 gen
✦ claude designer · generation 15 · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 6.886, g1 6.807, g2 6.552, g3 6.547, g4 6.547, g5 6.466, g6 6.412, g7 6.412, g8 6.412, g9 6.291, g10 6.291, g11 6.274, g12 6.274, g13 6.274, g14 6.274 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 5 generation(s)

CURRENT ELITES (best first):
fitness 6.274 Wh/km-agg, frame 92 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.3072, "arm_width_scale": 0.76, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 31.8, "rear_sweep_deg": 39.3318, "plate_length_scale": 0.97, "plate_width_scale": 1.0, "deck_gap": 0.026, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.7, "material": 0.0} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.3, "cold_headwind": 4.0, "crosswind": 3.86, "gusty_light": 3.7, "hot_thin": 3.52, "storm": 4.82}

fitness 6.308 Wh/km-agg, frame 94 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.264, "arm_width_scale": 0.8556, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 31.9039, "rear_sweep_deg": 35.9787, "plate_length_scale": 0.9731, "plate_width_scale": 0.9864, "deck_gap": 0.0262, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.7368, "material": 0.051} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.32, "cold_headwind": 4.03, "crosswind": 3.88, "gusty_light": 3.72, "hot_thin": 3.54, "storm": 4.84}
fitness 6.308 Wh/km-agg, frame 93 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.3096, "arm_width_scale": 0.7559, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 35.4827, "rear_sweep_deg": 36.5954, "plate_length_scale": 0.9803, "plate_width_scale": 0.9817, "deck_gap": 0.025, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.7568, "material": 0.0} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.32, "cold_headwind": 4.03, "crosswind": 3.88, "gusty_light": 3.72, "hot_thin": 3.54, "storm": 4.84}

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g0 `9be55419` fitness 7.598 Wh/km-agg — was: Heavy-duty ASA build with thick plates and wide arms near stock sweep for maximum stiffness/durability robustness in storm gusts, accepting a mass penalty.
- g0 `9f8cb660` fitness 6.908 Wh/km-agg — was: Narrow low-drag arms with a tilted battery to cut frontal area for cold_headwind/crosswind, keeping sweep change modest and deck_gap raised for safety margin.
- g3 `4ac62300` fitness 7.026 Wh/km-agg — was: Near-stock sweep (small deltas, clear of the tongue-collision minimum) with plate scales pushed well above the 0.95 web-crush threshold and a thicker printed PLA+ build to test a tougher, mass-tolerant alternative to carbon.
- g3 `a76a6566` fitness 6.705 Wh/km-agg — was: Wider front/rear sweep for a broader stance to help crosswind/storm stability, paired with generously enlarged (not merely stock) plate scales and deck_gap to safely clear the tongue-bolt and web-crush failures seen at extreme sweep.
- g3 `edbeb491` fitness 6.977 Wh/km-agg — was: Moderate-stiffness storm specialist: wider/thicker arms with less waist-narrowing and tough PA12-CF material plus generous deck_gap, betting that structural robustness (not raw mass-adding bulk like earlier failed 'storm-hardened' attempts) trims the worst-case storm penalty.
- g6 `0744daaf` fitness 6.943 Wh/km-agg — was: Wide-stance crosswind/storm specialist that pushes both sweeps ~6-7 deg past stock for a broader footprint, with plate_length/width_scale raised well past 1.0 (per the ~0.1-per-5-deg rule) to keep the tongue bolts seated and the plate webs thick.
- g6 `7b8317ed` fitness 7.262 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-stiffness specialist: a moderately wider stance (5 deg beyond stock both sweeps, plate scales raised per the sweep-deviation rule) with less waist-narrowing and thicker arms/plates than the elites for gust stiffness, while staying on lightweight carbon to avoid the mass penalty that sank earlier 'storm bruiser' attempts.
- g6 `ed5a193d` fitness 7.377 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-hardened stiff-arm design with arm_length_scale raised to 1.20 (vs the failed 0.88) to restore rotor tip clearance at a near-stock sweep, plus generous plate margins to guard the tongue-bolt/web-crush zones.
- g9 `01f642b6` fitness 6.523 Wh/km-agg — was: Isolate the effect of a genuinely wide stance (front/rear sweep pushed well past stock) from the thick-arm mass penalty that sank earlier wide-stance attempts, by pairing it with thin floor-waist arms and plate scales raised generously to keep the tongue bolts and webs safely clear.
- g9 `2ab37da5` fitness 6.429 Wh/km-agg — was: Replaces the failed vector by raising deck_gap well past the elite range (0.034 vs 0.028) and restoring a stiffer plate_thickness_scale (1.1 vs the failed 0.9) to push the rotor disk clear of the deck/battery stack while keeping near-stock sweeps and carbon construction.
- g9 `8d9fd4a0` fitness 6.291 Wh/km-agg — was: Push arm_length_scale near its max (lowers disk loading) with thin low-drag arms and deliberately cut plate_thickness_scale to 0.75 (below any elite) to shed mass for hot_thin/crosswind, keeping sweep essentially at stock so plate scales need no compensation.
- g12 `17da73c8` fitness 6.333 Wh/km-agg — was: Re-test the raised deck_gap (0.036, well past the 0.023 FC-stack minimum) for extra rotor/deck clearance margin, again pairing stock sweep with plate_length/width_scale at 1.00/1.03 to avoid the web-crush failure that killed the prior attempt at 0.96/0.96.
- g12 `af149969` fitness 6.355 Wh/km-agg — was: Isolate the untested battery_wedge_deg dimension (all elites sit at 0.0) by tilting the battery 12 deg to cut frontal area for cold_headwind/crosswind, otherwise cloning the elite recipe's proven arm/plate/material values exactly.
- g12 `ddf19b53` fitness 6.311 Wh/km-agg — was: Re-test extreme arm_length_scale (1.35 max, lower disk loading) at stock sweep, this time with plate_length/width_scale raised to 1.00/1.03 (above the elite's barely-passing 0.97/1.00) to keep the tongue-bolt web safely above the 1.90mm stock/80% floor.
- g13 `197c2380` fitness 7.025 Wh/km-agg — was: Compact stiff-arm storm specialist replacing the failed short-arm attempt: arm_length_scale raised from the failed 0.78 to 1.2 (within the tip-clearance-safe range the elites themselves use) while keeping the fat, thick, low-narrowing arms and thicker plates that trade mass for storm stiffness the elite family has abandoned.
- g13 `3844ac93` fitness 6.757 Wh/km-agg — was: Wide-stance crosswind/storm pivot replacing the failed extreme-sweep attempt: sweep deltas trimmed from 11.6/14 deg to a safer 6.6/8 deg past stock and plate_length/width_scale pushed to 1.10/1.15 (well beyond the ~0.1-per-5-deg rule) to keep the tongue bolts seated on the main plate.
- g13 `e28f0289` fitness 6.840 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the elite family's tight-deck-gap/no-wedge/floor-thickness trait: push deck_gap near its max for maximum rotor/deck clearance, tilt the battery near its max to cut frontal area, and raise plate_thickness_scale well above the floor for a stiffer core, while holding sweep at stock and arms moderate so none of these three untested dimensions gets confounded with a sweep or mass penalty.
- g14 `03a2ffd5` fitness 6.991 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family has converged on a minimum-mass/minimum-drag recipe (thin floor-thickness arms, near-stock sweep, tight deck gap, carbon) that has exhausted every free-mass knob without denting the storm/crosswind penalty, so this pivot abandons near-stock sweep for a genuinely wide stance (front 40, rear 48 deg) with plate scales grown per the sweep-deviation rule to test whether real aerodynami
- g14 `19c8d082` INVALID (arm tip deflection (pet_cf)) — was: Every elite uses material near 0.0 (pure carbon) because prior non-carbon attempts bundled the material switch with a heavy 'storm bruiser' stance that ate the mass budget, so this pivot isolates the material axis alone -- keep the proven elite-shaped long-arm, low-drag, near-stock-sweep geometry intact but swap to PET-CF (0.40) with plate_thickness_scale raised to 0.90 to meet the printed-materia
- g14 `e8b47537` fitness 6.371 Wh/km-agg — was: Re-test the untested battery_wedge_deg=14 frontal-area-reduction dimension, fixing the rotor-tip-clearance failure by raising arm_length_scale from the failed 0.9 to a safe 1.2 while leaving sweep, plate scales and material untouched so only the battery tilt is isolated.

SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 5 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 470c4fea — Every elite also sits at the plate_thickness_scale floor (0.7-0.76) with a tight deck_gap (0.025-0.026) -- deck/standoff stiffness and rotor-to-deck clearance under gust loading have never been tested independently of arm geometry or a mass-adding stance change, so this pivot keeps the elite's low-drag floor-thin arms and stock sweep untouched but pushes plate_thickness_scale near its max and deckflew at 6.574 Wh/km
  • candidate a55c3afa — Storm-stiffness pivot away from the elite family's thin/minimum-mass arms (wide arm_width=1.4, no waist narrowing, thick arm_thickness=0.007, carbon) that failed only on rotor/deck clearance -- fixed by raising deck_gap from the failed 0.027 to 0.04, well clear of the stack.flew at 7.773 Wh/km
  • candidate 4e66ffd7 — Re-isolate the untested tough ASA material (0.75) against the all-carbon elite family, keeping the same generous arm geometry as the failed attempt but raising deck_gap to 0.042 and trimming plate_thickness_scale to 0.85 (still above the printed-material 0.8 floor) to clear the deck/battery stack.invalid — arm tip deflection (petg)
  • candidate e69b2732 — rejected pre-flight (rotor too close to deck/battery) — The elite family shares thin/narrow/floor-thickness arms (width 0.75-0.86, waist 0.55, thickness 0.004) with near-stock sweep -- storm has stayed pinned at 4.82-4.84 Wh/km across all of them despite every other scenario improving, suggesting arm flex under gust loading (not mass or drag) is now the binding constraint on storm; this isolates pure arm structural stiffness (max width, max waist/no-na
  • candidate 239d2504 — rejected pre-flight (rotor too close to deck/battery) — Every elite and every prior material pivot has stayed at material~0.0 (carbon) because past non-carbon attempts bundled the switch with a heavy wide-stance or thin-arm combo that failed on tip deflection or web thickness; this isolates a genuinely tougher thermoplastic (PETG, 0.75) at stock sweep and elite-like plate scale, with arm width/waist pulled back from the elite's drag-optimized extremes

✦ claude designer · generation 14 · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 6.886, g1 6.807, g2 6.552, g3 6.547, g4 6.547, g5 6.466, g6 6.412, g7 6.412, g8 6.412, g9 6.291, g10 6.291, g11 6.274, g12 6.274, g13 6.274 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 4 generation(s)

CURRENT ELITES (best first):
fitness 6.274 Wh/km-agg, frame 92 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.3072, "arm_width_scale": 0.76, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 31.8, "rear_sweep_deg": 39.3318, "plate_length_scale": 0.97, "plate_width_scale": 1.0, "deck_gap": 0.026, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.7, "material": 0.0} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.3, "cold_headwind": 4.0, "crosswind": 3.86, "gusty_light": 3.7, "hot_thin": 3.52, "storm": 4.82}
fitness 6.286 Wh/km-agg, frame 92 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.2981, "arm_width_scale": 0.75, "arm_waist_scale": 0.5858, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 33.4578, "rear_sweep_deg": 36.2882, "plate_length_scale": 0.9697, "plate_width_scale": 0.9972, "deck_gap": 0.026, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.7, "material": 0.1202} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.31, "cold_headwind": 4.01, "crosswind": 3.87, "gusty_light": 3.71, "hot_thin": 3.53, "storm": 4.82}
fitness 6.291 Wh/km-agg, frame 93 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.3, "arm_width_scale": 0.76, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 31.8, "rear_sweep_deg": 36.3, "plate_length_scale": 0.97, "plate_width_scale": 1.0, "deck_gap": 0.026, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.75, "material": 0.05} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.31, "cold_headwind": 4.02, "crosswind": 3.87, "gusty_light": 3.71, "hot_thin": 3.53, "storm": 4.83}
fitness 6.308 Wh/km-agg, frame 94 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.264, "arm_width_scale": 0.8556, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 31.9039, "rear_sweep_deg": 35.9787, "plate_length_scale": 0.9731, "plate_width_scale": 0.9864, "deck_gap": 0.0262, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.7368, "material": 0.051} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.32, "cold_headwind": 4.03, "crosswind": 3.88, "gusty_light": 3.72, "hot_thin": 3.54, "storm": 4.84}
fitness 6.308 Wh/km-agg, frame 93 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.3096, "arm_width_scale": 0.7559, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 35.4827, "rear_sweep_deg": 36.5954, "plate_length_scale": 0.9803, "plate_width_scale": 0.9817, "deck_gap": 0.025, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.7568, "material": 0.0} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.32, "cold_headwind": 4.03, "crosswind": 3.88, "gusty_light": 3.72, "hot_thin": 3.54, "storm": 4.84}

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g0 `59b9a52c` fitness 7.127 Wh/km-agg — was: Compact, wide, stiff-armed storm-hardened design with a modest sweep shift and generous deck_gap/plate scale margin to clear the prior rotor-to-deck failure.
- g0 `7792e425` fitness 6.886 Wh/km-agg — was: Long low-disk-loading arms at exact stock sweep with a safely-oversized plate footprint and carbon construction, targeting hot_thin efficiency without touching the plate-web failure zone.
- g0 `89ffb906` fitness 8.479 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-hardened stiff bruiser: wide thick arms, long wheelbase and thick carbon plates trade mass for structural robustness, targeting the storm scenario that dominates worst-case fitness.
- g0 `9be55419` fitness 7.598 Wh/km-agg — was: Heavy-duty ASA build with thick plates and wide arms near stock sweep for maximum stiffness/durability robustness in storm gusts, accepting a mass penalty.
- g0 `9f8cb660` fitness 6.908 Wh/km-agg — was: Narrow low-drag arms with a tilted battery to cut frontal area for cold_headwind/crosswind, keeping sweep change modest and deck_gap raised for safety margin.
- g3 `4ac62300` fitness 7.026 Wh/km-agg — was: Near-stock sweep (small deltas, clear of the tongue-collision minimum) with plate scales pushed well above the 0.95 web-crush threshold and a thicker printed PLA+ build to test a tougher, mass-tolerant alternative to carbon.
- g3 `a76a6566` fitness 6.705 Wh/km-agg — was: Wider front/rear sweep for a broader stance to help crosswind/storm stability, paired with generously enlarged (not merely stock) plate scales and deck_gap to safely clear the tongue-bolt and web-crush failures seen at extreme sweep.
- g3 `edbeb491` fitness 6.977 Wh/km-agg — was: Moderate-stiffness storm specialist: wider/thicker arms with less waist-narrowing and tough PA12-CF material plus generous deck_gap, betting that structural robustness (not raw mass-adding bulk like earlier failed 'storm-hardened' attempts) trims the worst-case storm penalty.
- g6 `0744daaf` fitness 6.943 Wh/km-agg — was: Wide-stance crosswind/storm specialist that pushes both sweeps ~6-7 deg past stock for a broader footprint, with plate_length/width_scale raised well past 1.0 (per the ~0.1-per-5-deg rule) to keep the tongue bolts seated and the plate webs thick.
- g6 `7b8317ed` fitness 7.262 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-stiffness specialist: a moderately wider stance (5 deg beyond stock both sweeps, plate scales raised per the sweep-deviation rule) with less waist-narrowing and thicker arms/plates than the elites for gust stiffness, while staying on lightweight carbon to avoid the mass penalty that sank earlier 'storm bruiser' attempts.
- g6 `ed5a193d` fitness 7.377 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-hardened stiff-arm design with arm_length_scale raised to 1.20 (vs the failed 0.88) to restore rotor tip clearance at a near-stock sweep, plus generous plate margins to guard the tongue-bolt/web-crush zones.
- g9 `01f642b6` fitness 6.523 Wh/km-agg — was: Isolate the effect of a genuinely wide stance (front/rear sweep pushed well past stock) from the thick-arm mass penalty that sank earlier wide-stance attempts, by pairing it with thin floor-waist arms and plate scales raised generously to keep the tongue bolts and webs safely clear.
- g9 `2ab37da5` fitness 6.429 Wh/km-agg — was: Replaces the failed vector by raising deck_gap well past the elite range (0.034 vs 0.028) and restoring a stiffer plate_thickness_scale (1.1 vs the failed 0.9) to push the rotor disk clear of the deck/battery stack while keeping near-stock sweeps and carbon construction.
- g9 `8d9fd4a0` fitness 6.291 Wh/km-agg — was: Push arm_length_scale near its max (lowers disk loading) with thin low-drag arms and deliberately cut plate_thickness_scale to 0.75 (below any elite) to shed mass for hot_thin/crosswind, keeping sweep essentially at stock so plate scales need no compensation.
- g12 `17da73c8` fitness 6.333 Wh/km-agg — was: Re-test the raised deck_gap (0.036, well past the 0.023 FC-stack minimum) for extra rotor/deck clearance margin, again pairing stock sweep with plate_length/width_scale at 1.00/1.03 to avoid the web-crush failure that killed the prior attempt at 0.96/0.96.
- g12 `af149969` fitness 6.355 Wh/km-agg — was: Isolate the untested battery_wedge_deg dimension (all elites sit at 0.0) by tilting the battery 12 deg to cut frontal area for cold_headwind/crosswind, otherwise cloning the elite recipe's proven arm/plate/material values exactly.
- g12 `ddf19b53` fitness 6.311 Wh/km-agg — was: Re-test extreme arm_length_scale (1.35 max, lower disk loading) at stock sweep, this time with plate_length/width_scale raised to 1.00/1.03 (above the elite's barely-passing 0.97/1.00) to keep the tongue-bolt web safely above the 1.90mm stock/80% floor.
- g13 `197c2380` fitness 7.025 Wh/km-agg — was: Compact stiff-arm storm specialist replacing the failed short-arm attempt: arm_length_scale raised from the failed 0.78 to 1.2 (within the tip-clearance-safe range the elites themselves use) while keeping the fat, thick, low-narrowing arms and thicker plates that trade mass for storm stiffness the elite family has abandoned.
- g13 `3844ac93` fitness 6.757 Wh/km-agg — was: Wide-stance crosswind/storm pivot replacing the failed extreme-sweep attempt: sweep deltas trimmed from 11.6/14 deg to a safer 6.6/8 deg past stock and plate_length/width_scale pushed to 1.10/1.15 (well beyond the ~0.1-per-5-deg rule) to keep the tongue bolts seated on the main plate.
- g13 `e28f0289` fitness 6.840 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the elite family's tight-deck-gap/no-wedge/floor-thickness trait: push deck_gap near its max for maximum rotor/deck clearance, tilt the battery near its max to cut frontal area, and raise plate_thickness_scale well above the floor for a stiffer core, while holding sweep at stock and arms moderate so none of these three untested dimensions gets confounded with a sweep or mass penalty.

RECENT INVALID-DESIGN REASONS (histogram): {"plate web too thin (plate_main 0.32 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "deck gap too small for FC/ESC stack": 8, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.66 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.06 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm tongue bolts miss the main plate": 10, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.48 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.59 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "rotor too close to deck/battery": 11, "arm resonance with rotor 1P": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_top 1.25 mm, stock 1.75 mm)": 2, "rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)": 7, "plate web too thin (plate_mid 1.11 mm, stock 1.47 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.84 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm root tongues collide on the main plate": 7, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.78 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pet_cf (1.5 < 1.6 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.85 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.97 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.4 < 1.6 mm)": 4, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.73 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm tip deflection (pa12_cf)": 5, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.44 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.5 < 1.6 mm)": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.37 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.6 < 1.6 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.24 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.23 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.18 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_top 1.02 mm, stock 1.75 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.37 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.20 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_top 1.00 mm, stock 1.75 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.99 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1}


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 4 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 03a2ffd5 — The elite family has converged on a minimum-mass/minimum-drag recipe (thin floor-thickness arms, near-stock sweep, tight deck gap, carbon) that has exhausted every free-mass knob without denting the storm/crosswind penalty, so this pivot abandons near-stock sweep for a genuinely wide stance (front 40, rear 48 deg) with plate scales grown per the sweep-deviation rule to test whether real aerodynamiflew at 6.991 Wh/km
  • candidate 19c8d082 — Every elite uses material near 0.0 (pure carbon) because prior non-carbon attempts bundled the material switch with a heavy 'storm bruiser' stance that ate the mass budget, so this pivot isolates the material axis alone -- keep the proven elite-shaped long-arm, low-drag, near-stock-sweep geometry intact but swap to PET-CF (0.40) with plate_thickness_scale raised to 0.90 to meet the printed-materiainvalid — arm tip deflection (pet_cf)
  • candidate e8b47537 — Re-test the untested battery_wedge_deg=14 frontal-area-reduction dimension, fixing the rotor-tip-clearance failure by raising arm_length_scale from the failed 0.9 to a safe 1.2 while leaving sweep, plate scales and material untouched so only the battery tilt is isolated.flew at 6.371 Wh/km
  • candidate e1f8e520 — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Every elite sits at arm_length_scale ~1.3 (low disk loading, long low-drag arms) with battery_wedge_deg untouched at 0, so this pivot flips both traits at once -- a compact, higher-disk-loading frame (arm_length_scale 0.90) paired with an aggressive 14 deg battery tilt to slash frontal area -- isolating whether a shorter/lighter airframe plus real drag-shedding tilt beats the long-arm family on co

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 6.886, g1 6.807, g2 6.552, g3 6.547, g4 6.547, g5 6.466, g6 6.412, g7 6.412, g8 6.412, g9 6.291, g10 6.291, g11 6.274, g12 6.274 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 3 generation(s)

CURRENT ELITES (best first):
fitness 6.274 Wh/km-agg, frame 92 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.3072, "arm_width_scale": 0.76, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 31.8, "rear_sweep_deg": 39.3318, "plate_length_scale": 0.97, "plate_width_scale": 1.0, "deck_gap": 0.026, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.7, "material": 0.0} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.3, "cold_headwind": 4.0, "crosswind": 3.86, "gusty_light": 3.7, "hot_thin": 3.52, "storm": 4.82}

fitness 6.308 Wh/km-agg, frame 94 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.264, "arm_width_scale": 0.8556, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 31.9039, "rear_sweep_deg": 35.9787, "plate_length_scale": 0.9731, "plate_width_scale": 0.9864, "deck_gap": 0.0262, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.7368, "material": 0.051} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.32, "cold_headwind": 4.03, "crosswind": 3.88, "gusty_light": 3.72, "hot_thin": 3.54, "storm": 4.84}
fitness 6.308 Wh/km-agg, frame 93 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.3096, "arm_width_scale": 0.7559, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 35.4827, "rear_sweep_deg": 36.5954, "plate_length_scale": 0.9803, "plate_width_scale": 0.9817, "deck_gap": 0.025, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.7568, "material": 0.0} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.32, "cold_headwind": 4.03, "crosswind": 3.88, "gusty_light": 3.72, "hot_thin": 3.54, "storm": 4.84}

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g0 `59b9a52c` fitness 7.127 Wh/km-agg — was: Compact, wide, stiff-armed storm-hardened design with a modest sweep shift and generous deck_gap/plate scale margin to clear the prior rotor-to-deck failure.
- g0 `7792e425` fitness 6.886 Wh/km-agg — was: Long low-disk-loading arms at exact stock sweep with a safely-oversized plate footprint and carbon construction, targeting hot_thin efficiency without touching the plate-web failure zone.
- g0 `89ffb906` fitness 8.479 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-hardened stiff bruiser: wide thick arms, long wheelbase and thick carbon plates trade mass for structural robustness, targeting the storm scenario that dominates worst-case fitness.
- g0 `9be55419` fitness 7.598 Wh/km-agg — was: Heavy-duty ASA build with thick plates and wide arms near stock sweep for maximum stiffness/durability robustness in storm gusts, accepting a mass penalty.
- g0 `9f8cb660` fitness 6.908 Wh/km-agg — was: Narrow low-drag arms with a tilted battery to cut frontal area for cold_headwind/crosswind, keeping sweep change modest and deck_gap raised for safety margin.
- g3 `4ac62300` fitness 7.026 Wh/km-agg — was: Near-stock sweep (small deltas, clear of the tongue-collision minimum) with plate scales pushed well above the 0.95 web-crush threshold and a thicker printed PLA+ build to test a tougher, mass-tolerant alternative to carbon.
- g3 `a76a6566` fitness 6.705 Wh/km-agg — was: Wider front/rear sweep for a broader stance to help crosswind/storm stability, paired with generously enlarged (not merely stock) plate scales and deck_gap to safely clear the tongue-bolt and web-crush failures seen at extreme sweep.
- g3 `edbeb491` fitness 6.977 Wh/km-agg — was: Moderate-stiffness storm specialist: wider/thicker arms with less waist-narrowing and tough PA12-CF material plus generous deck_gap, betting that structural robustness (not raw mass-adding bulk like earlier failed 'storm-hardened' attempts) trims the worst-case storm penalty.
- g6 `0744daaf` fitness 6.943 Wh/km-agg — was: Wide-stance crosswind/storm specialist that pushes both sweeps ~6-7 deg past stock for a broader footprint, with plate_length/width_scale raised well past 1.0 (per the ~0.1-per-5-deg rule) to keep the tongue bolts seated and the plate webs thick.
- g6 `7b8317ed` fitness 7.262 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-stiffness specialist: a moderately wider stance (5 deg beyond stock both sweeps, plate scales raised per the sweep-deviation rule) with less waist-narrowing and thicker arms/plates than the elites for gust stiffness, while staying on lightweight carbon to avoid the mass penalty that sank earlier 'storm bruiser' attempts.
- g6 `ed5a193d` fitness 7.377 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-hardened stiff-arm design with arm_length_scale raised to 1.20 (vs the failed 0.88) to restore rotor tip clearance at a near-stock sweep, plus generous plate margins to guard the tongue-bolt/web-crush zones.
- g9 `01f642b6` fitness 6.523 Wh/km-agg — was: Isolate the effect of a genuinely wide stance (front/rear sweep pushed well past stock) from the thick-arm mass penalty that sank earlier wide-stance attempts, by pairing it with thin floor-waist arms and plate scales raised generously to keep the tongue bolts and webs safely clear.
- g9 `2ab37da5` fitness 6.429 Wh/km-agg — was: Replaces the failed vector by raising deck_gap well past the elite range (0.034 vs 0.028) and restoring a stiffer plate_thickness_scale (1.1 vs the failed 0.9) to push the rotor disk clear of the deck/battery stack while keeping near-stock sweeps and carbon construction.
- g9 `8d9fd4a0` fitness 6.291 Wh/km-agg — was: Push arm_length_scale near its max (lowers disk loading) with thin low-drag arms and deliberately cut plate_thickness_scale to 0.75 (below any elite) to shed mass for hot_thin/crosswind, keeping sweep essentially at stock so plate scales need no compensation.
- g12 `17da73c8` fitness 6.333 Wh/km-agg — was: Re-test the raised deck_gap (0.036, well past the 0.023 FC-stack minimum) for extra rotor/deck clearance margin, again pairing stock sweep with plate_length/width_scale at 1.00/1.03 to avoid the web-crush failure that killed the prior attempt at 0.96/0.96.
- g12 `af149969` fitness 6.355 Wh/km-agg — was: Isolate the untested battery_wedge_deg dimension (all elites sit at 0.0) by tilting the battery 12 deg to cut frontal area for cold_headwind/crosswind, otherwise cloning the elite recipe's proven arm/plate/material values exactly.
- g12 `ddf19b53` fitness 6.311 Wh/km-agg — was: Re-test extreme arm_length_scale (1.35 max, lower disk loading) at stock sweep, this time with plate_length/width_scale raised to 1.00/1.03 (above the elite's barely-passing 0.97/1.00) to keep the tongue-bolt web safely above the 1.90mm stock/80% floor.

RECENT INVALID-DESIGN REASONS (histogram): {"plate web too thin (plate_main 0.32 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "deck gap too small for FC/ESC stack": 6, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.66 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.06 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm tongue bolts miss the main plate": 9, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.48 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.59 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "rotor too close to deck/battery": 11, "arm resonance with rotor 1P": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_top 1.25 mm, stock 1.75 mm)": 2, "rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)": 7, "plate web too thin (plate_mid 1.11 mm, stock 1.47 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.84 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm root tongues collide on the main plate": 7, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.78 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pet_cf (1.5 < 1.6 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.85 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.97 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.4 < 1.6 mm)": 4, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.73 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm tip deflection (pa12_cf)": 5, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.44 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.5 < 1.6 mm)": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.37 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.6 < 1.6 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.24 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.23 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.18 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_top 1.02 mm, stock 1.75 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.37 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.20 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_top 1.00 mm, stock 1.75 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.99 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1}


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 3 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate e28f0289 — Abandon the elite family's tight-deck-gap/no-wedge/floor-thickness trait: push deck_gap near its max for maximum rotor/deck clearance, tilt the battery near its max to cut frontal area, and raise plate_thickness_scale well above the floor for a stiffer core, while holding sweep at stock and arms moderate so none of these three untested dimensions gets confounded with a sweep or mass penalty.flew at 6.840 Wh/km
  • candidate 197c2380 — Compact stiff-arm storm specialist replacing the failed short-arm attempt: arm_length_scale raised from the failed 0.78 to 1.2 (within the tip-clearance-safe range the elites themselves use) while keeping the fat, thick, low-narrowing arms and thicker plates that trade mass for storm stiffness the elite family has abandoned.flew at 7.025 Wh/km
  • candidate 3844ac93 — Wide-stance crosswind/storm pivot replacing the failed extreme-sweep attempt: sweep deltas trimmed from 11.6/14 deg to a safer 6.6/8 deg past stock and plate_length/width_scale pushed to 1.10/1.15 (well beyond the ~0.1-per-5-deg rule) to keep the tongue bolts seated on the main plate.flew at 6.757 Wh/km
  • candidate 87fd3524 — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Abandon the elite family's max-arm-length/thin-everything trait entirely: shrink to near-minimum arm length for higher disk loading but restore arm width/waist/thickness toward the middle of the range for real structural stiffness, testing whether a compact stiff core beats the plateaued long-thin-arm minimalism on storm without paying its usual mass tax.
  • candidate a8ab6930 — rejected pre-flight (arm tongue bolts miss the main plate) — Abandon the elite family's stock-sweep constraint: push both sweeps far beyond stock for a genuinely wide stance (unlike prior failed wide-stance attempts that piled on thick arms), while keeping arms thin/light for low drag/mass and raising plate_length/width_scale well past the web-crush floor so the tongue bolts and webs stay safely seated, isolating stance-width benefit from the mass penalty t

inputs
✦ claude designer · scheduled round · 3 design(s) injected, 2 rejected pre-flight
elites carried over unchanged2
claude-designed3

random immigrants1

✦ claude designer · generation 12 · scheduled round · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 6.886, g1 6.807, g2 6.552, g3 6.547, g4 6.547, g5 6.466, g6 6.412, g7 6.412, g8 6.412, g9 6.291, g10 6.291, g11 6.274 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 2 generation(s)

CURRENT ELITES (best first):

- g3 `4ac62300` fitness 7.026 Wh/km-agg — was: Near-stock sweep (small deltas, clear of the tongue-collision minimum) with plate scales pushed well above the 0.95 web-crush threshold and a thicker printed PLA+ build to test a tougher, mass-tolerant alternative to carbon.
- g3 `a76a6566` fitness 6.705 Wh/km-agg — was: Wider front/rear sweep for a broader stance to help crosswind/storm stability, paired with generously enlarged (not merely stock) plate scales and deck_gap to safely clear the tongue-bolt and web-crush failures seen at extreme sweep.
- g3 `edbeb491` fitness 6.977 Wh/km-agg — was: Moderate-stiffness storm specialist: wider/thicker arms with less waist-narrowing and tough PA12-CF material plus generous deck_gap, betting that structural robustness (not raw mass-adding bulk like earlier failed 'storm-hardened' attempts) trims the worst-case storm penalty.
- g6 `0744daaf` fitness 6.943 Wh/km-agg — was: Wide-stance crosswind/storm specialist that pushes both sweeps ~6-7 deg past stock for a broader footprint, with plate_length/width_scale raised well past 1.0 (per the ~0.1-per-5-deg rule) to keep the tongue bolts seated and the plate webs thick.
- g6 `7b8317ed` fitness 7.262 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-stiffness specialist: a moderately wider stance (5 deg beyond stock both sweeps, plate scales raised per the sweep-deviation rule) with less waist-narrowing and thicker arms/plates than the elites for gust stiffness, while staying on lightweight carbon to avoid the mass penalty that sank earlier 'storm bruiser' attempts.
- g6 `ed5a193d` fitness 7.377 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-hardened stiff-arm design with arm_length_scale raised to 1.20 (vs the failed 0.88) to restore rotor tip clearance at a near-stock sweep, plus generous plate margins to guard the tongue-bolt/web-crush zones.
- g9 `01f642b6` fitness 6.523 Wh/km-agg — was: Isolate the effect of a genuinely wide stance (front/rear sweep pushed well past stock) from the thick-arm mass penalty that sank earlier wide-stance attempts, by pairing it with thin floor-waist arms and plate scales raised generously to keep the tongue bolts and webs safely clear.
- g9 `2ab37da5` fitness 6.429 Wh/km-agg — was: Replaces the failed vector by raising deck_gap well past the elite range (0.034 vs 0.028) and restoring a stiffer plate_thickness_scale (1.1 vs the failed 0.9) to push the rotor disk clear of the deck/battery stack while keeping near-stock sweeps and carbon construction.
- g9 `8d9fd4a0` fitness 6.291 Wh/km-agg — was: Push arm_length_scale near its max (lowers disk loading) with thin low-drag arms and deliberately cut plate_thickness_scale to 0.75 (below any elite) to shed mass for hot_thin/crosswind, keeping sweep essentially at stock so plate scales need no compensation.

RECENT INVALID-DESIGN REASONS (histogram): {"plate web too thin (plate_main 0.32 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "deck gap too small for FC/ESC stack": 6, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.66 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.06 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm tongue bolts miss the main plate": 9, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.48 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.59 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "rotor too close to deck/battery": 10, "arm resonance with rotor 1P": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_top 1.25 mm, stock 1.75 mm)": 2, "rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)": 7, "plate web too thin (plate_mid 1.11 mm, stock 1.47 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.84 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm root tongues collide on the main plate": 6, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.78 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pet_cf (1.5 < 1.6 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.85 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.97 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.4 < 1.6 mm)": 3, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.73 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm tip deflection (pa12_cf)": 5, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.44 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.5 < 1.6 mm)": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.37 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.6 < 1.6 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.24 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.23 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.18 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_top 1.02 mm, stock 1.75 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.37 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.20 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_top 1.00 mm, stock 1.75 mm)": 1}


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

Propose exactly 3 NEW genome vectors that are meaningfully DIFFERENT from the elites and from each other — design hypotheses, not small perturbations. Build on what the trajectory and your earlier proposals' outcomes show worked; avoid repeating what failed. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate af149969 — Isolate the untested battery_wedge_deg dimension (all elites sit at 0.0) by tilting the battery 12 deg to cut frontal area for cold_headwind/crosswind, otherwise cloning the elite recipe's proven arm/plate/material values exactly.flew at 6.355 Wh/km
  • candidate ddf19b53 — Re-test extreme arm_length_scale (1.35 max, lower disk loading) at stock sweep, this time with plate_length/width_scale raised to 1.00/1.03 (above the elite's barely-passing 0.97/1.00) to keep the tongue-bolt web safely above the 1.90mm stock/80% floor.flew at 6.311 Wh/km
  • candidate 17da73c8 — Re-test the raised deck_gap (0.036, well past the 0.023 FC-stack minimum) for extra rotor/deck clearance margin, again pairing stock sweep with plate_length/width_scale at 1.00/1.03 to avoid the web-crush failure that killed the prior attempt at 0.96/0.96.flew at 6.333 Wh/km
  • candidate a2cca76f — rejected pre-flight (plate web too thin (plate_main 1.25 mm, stock 1.90 mm)) — Push arm_length_scale to its true maximum (1.35, beyond any elite's ~1.30) to further lower disk loading and hover power, while keeping arms at floor width/waist/thickness, sweeps at exact stock (zero plate-scale compensation needed), and floor plate thickness/carbon to stay in the proven low-mass recipe.
  • candidate 7bc9fa75 — rejected pre-flight (plate web too thin (plate_main 1.25 mm, stock 1.90 mm)) — Isolate deck_gap as a storm-clearance lever by raising it to 0.036 (well past the elite ~0.026 and the FC-stack minimum) while holding plate_thickness_scale at the floor and sweeps at exact stock, unlike the earlier failed deck_gap attempt that confounded the effect with added plate mass.

✦ claude designer · generation 9 · scheduled round · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 6.886, g1 6.807, g2 6.552, g3 6.547, g4 6.547, g5 6.466, g6 6.412, g7 6.412, g8 6.412 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 2 generation(s)

CURRENT ELITES (best first):
fitness 6.412 Wh/km-agg, frame 108 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.0121, "arm_width_scale": 0.8495, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 33.3446, "rear_sweep_deg": 37.576, "plate_length_scale": 0.9653, "plate_width_scale": 1.0537, "deck_gap": 0.0302, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 1.3574, "material": 0.1089} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.39, "cold_headwind": 4.07, "crosswind": 3.94, "gusty_light": 3.78, "hot_thin": 3.62, "storm": 4.92}
fitness 6.454 Wh/km-agg, frame 107 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.1836, "arm_width_scale": 0.7548, "arm_waist_scale": 0.5504, "arm_thickness": 0.0041, "front_sweep_deg": 31.4905, "rear_sweep_deg": 34.0, "plate_length_scale": 1.0706, "plate_width_scale": 1.0587, "deck_gap": 0.0312, "battery_wedge_deg": 1.8637, "plate_thickness_scale": 1.057, "material": 0.0648} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.4, "cold_headwind": 4.11, "crosswind": 3.97, "gusty_light": 3.8, "hot_thin": 3.63, "storm": 4.95}
fitness 6.461 Wh/km-agg, frame 107 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.1955, "arm_width_scale": 0.8684, "arm_waist_scale": 0.5523, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 33.2426, "rear_sweep_deg": 35.7588, "plate_length_scale": 1.1303, "plate_width_scale": 1.0585, "deck_gap": 0.0301, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.1364, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.9624, "material": 0.0385} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.4, "cold_headwind": 4.12, "crosswind": 3.97, "gusty_light": 3.81, "hot_thin": 3.63, "storm": 4.96}
fitness 6.466 Wh/km-agg, frame 109 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.1802, "arm_width_scale": 0.8568, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.0041, "front_sweep_deg": 31.481, "rear_sweep_deg": 34.9638, "plate_length_scale": 1.0686, "plate_width_scale": 1.0587, "deck_gap": 0.0302, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 1.0569, "material": 0.0659} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.41, "cold_headwind": 4.12, "crosswind": 3.98, "gusty_light": 3.81, "hot_thin": 3.63, "storm": 4.96}
fitness 6.478 Wh/km-agg, frame 113 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.0278, "arm_width_scale": 0.8776, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 33.3641, "rear_sweep_deg": 37.5706, "plate_length_scale": 0.9759, "plate_width_scale": 1.0538, "deck_gap": 0.0328, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 1.4402, "material": 0.0976} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.42, "cold_headwind": 4.11, "crosswind": 3.98, "gusty_light": 3.82, "hot_thin": 3.65, "storm": 4.97}

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g0 `59b9a52c` fitness 7.127 Wh/km-agg — was: Compact, wide, stiff-armed storm-hardened design with a modest sweep shift and generous deck_gap/plate scale margin to clear the prior rotor-to-deck failure.
- g0 `7792e425` fitness 6.886 Wh/km-agg — was: Long low-disk-loading arms at exact stock sweep with a safely-oversized plate footprint and carbon construction, targeting hot_thin efficiency without touching the plate-web failure zone.
- g0 `89ffb906` fitness 8.479 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-hardened stiff bruiser: wide thick arms, long wheelbase and thick carbon plates trade mass for structural robustness, targeting the storm scenario that dominates worst-case fitness.
- g0 `9be55419` fitness 7.598 Wh/km-agg — was: Heavy-duty ASA build with thick plates and wide arms near stock sweep for maximum stiffness/durability robustness in storm gusts, accepting a mass penalty.
- g0 `9f8cb660` fitness 6.908 Wh/km-agg — was: Narrow low-drag arms with a tilted battery to cut frontal area for cold_headwind/crosswind, keeping sweep change modest and deck_gap raised for safety margin.
- g3 `4ac62300` fitness 7.026 Wh/km-agg — was: Near-stock sweep (small deltas, clear of the tongue-collision minimum) with plate scales pushed well above the 0.95 web-crush threshold and a thicker printed PLA+ build to test a tougher, mass-tolerant alternative to carbon.
- g3 `a76a6566` fitness 6.705 Wh/km-agg — was: Wider front/rear sweep for a broader stance to help crosswind/storm stability, paired with generously enlarged (not merely stock) plate scales and deck_gap to safely clear the tongue-bolt and web-crush failures seen at extreme sweep.
- g3 `edbeb491` fitness 6.977 Wh/km-agg — was: Moderate-stiffness storm specialist: wider/thicker arms with less waist-narrowing and tough PA12-CF material plus generous deck_gap, betting that structural robustness (not raw mass-adding bulk like earlier failed 'storm-hardened' attempts) trims the worst-case storm penalty.
- g6 `0744daaf` fitness 6.943 Wh/km-agg — was: Wide-stance crosswind/storm specialist that pushes both sweeps ~6-7 deg past stock for a broader footprint, with plate_length/width_scale raised well past 1.0 (per the ~0.1-per-5-deg rule) to keep the tongue bolts seated and the plate webs thick.
- g6 `7b8317ed` fitness 7.262 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-stiffness specialist: a moderately wider stance (5 deg beyond stock both sweeps, plate scales raised per the sweep-deviation rule) with less waist-narrowing and thicker arms/plates than the elites for gust stiffness, while staying on lightweight carbon to avoid the mass penalty that sank earlier 'storm bruiser' attempts.
- g6 `ed5a193d` fitness 7.377 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-hardened stiff-arm design with arm_length_scale raised to 1.20 (vs the failed 0.88) to restore rotor tip clearance at a near-stock sweep, plus generous plate margins to guard the tongue-bolt/web-crush zones.

RECENT INVALID-DESIGN REASONS (histogram): {"plate web too thin (plate_main 0.32 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "deck gap too small for FC/ESC stack": 4, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.66 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.06 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm tongue bolts miss the main plate": 8, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.48 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.59 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "rotor too close to deck/battery": 7, "arm resonance with rotor 1P": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_top 1.25 mm, stock 1.75 mm)": 1, "rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)": 6, "plate web too thin (plate_mid 1.11 mm, stock 1.47 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.84 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm root tongues collide on the main plate": 6, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.78 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pet_cf (1.5 < 1.6 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.85 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.97 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.4 < 1.6 mm)": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.73 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm tip deflection (pa12_cf)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.44 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.5 < 1.6 mm)": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.37 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.6 < 1.6 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.24 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.23 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1}


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

Propose exactly 3 NEW genome vectors that are meaningfully DIFFERENT from the elites and from each other — design hypotheses, not small perturbations. Build on what the trajectory and your earlier proposals' outcomes show worked; avoid repeating what failed. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 8d9fd4a0 — Push arm_length_scale near its max (lowers disk loading) with thin low-drag arms and deliberately cut plate_thickness_scale to 0.75 (below any elite) to shed mass for hot_thin/crosswind, keeping sweep essentially at stock so plate scales need no compensation.flew at 6.291 Wh/km
  • candidate 01f642b6 — Isolate the effect of a genuinely wide stance (front/rear sweep pushed well past stock) from the thick-arm mass penalty that sank earlier wide-stance attempts, by pairing it with thin floor-waist arms and plate scales raised generously to keep the tongue bolts and webs safely clear.flew at 6.523 Wh/km
  • candidate 2ab37da5 — Replaces the failed vector by raising deck_gap well past the elite range (0.034 vs 0.028) and restoring a stiffer plate_thickness_scale (1.1 vs the failed 0.9) to push the rotor disk clear of the deck/battery stack while keeping near-stock sweeps and carbon construction.flew at 6.429 Wh/km
  • candidate 6921b5fe — rejected pre-flight (rotor too close to deck/battery) — Test whether a tough printed material (PA12-CF) at the minimum viable plate thickness can match carbon's efficiency on a lean, near-stock-sweep frame, unlike prior bulky printed-material attempts that added unnecessary mass.

inputs
✦ claude designer · scheduled round · 3 design(s) injected, 2 rejected pre-flight

✦ claude designer · generation 6 · scheduled round · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 6.886, g1 6.807, g2 6.552, g3 6.547, g4 6.547, g5 6.466

CURRENT ELITES (best first):
fitness 6.466 Wh/km-agg, frame 109 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.1802, "arm_width_scale": 0.8568, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.0041, "front_sweep_deg": 31.481, "rear_sweep_deg": 34.9638, "plate_length_scale": 1.0686, "plate_width_scale": 1.0587, "deck_gap": 0.0302, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 1.0569, "material": 0.0659} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.41, "cold_headwind": 4.12, "crosswind": 3.98, "gusty_light": 3.81, "hot_thin": 3.63, "storm": 4.96}
fitness 6.528 Wh/km-agg, frame 114 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.1896, "arm_width_scale": 0.8692, "arm_waist_scale": 0.5522, "arm_thickness": 0.0043, "front_sweep_deg": 33.2424, "rear_sweep_deg": 36.0119, "plate_length_scale": 1.0688, "plate_width_scale": 1.0587, "deck_gap": 0.0301, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.1155, "plate_thickness_scale": 1.1004, "material": 0.0} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.44, "cold_headwind": 4.16, "crosswind": 4.02, "gusty_light": 3.85, "hot_thin": 3.67, "storm": 5.01}
fitness 6.547 Wh/km-agg, frame 116 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.1896, "arm_width_scale": 0.8692, "arm_waist_scale": 0.5522, "arm_thickness": 0.0043, "front_sweep_deg": 31.7017, "rear_sweep_deg": 36.0119, "plate_length_scale": 1.0688, "plate_width_scale": 1.0587, "deck_gap": 0.0301, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.1155, "plate_thickness_scale": 1.1546, "material": 0.072} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.44, "cold_headwind": 4.17, "crosswind": 4.03, "gusty_light": 3.86, "hot_thin": 3.67, "storm": 5.03}
fitness 6.547 Wh/km-agg, frame 112 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.1869, "arm_width_scale": 0.8514, "arm_waist_scale": 0.7119, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 32.5276, "rear_sweep_deg": 35.4614, "plate_length_scale": 1.0668, "plate_width_scale": 1.062, "deck_gap": 0.0294, "battery_wedge_deg": 4.7339, "plate_thickness_scale": 1.053, "material": 0.0792} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.45, "cold_headwind": 4.18, "crosswind": 4.03, "gusty_light": 3.86, "hot_thin": 3.68, "storm": 5.02}
fitness 6.552 Wh/km-agg, frame 116 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 1.1874, "arm_width_scale": 0.8442, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.0045, "front_sweep_deg": 31.4716, "rear_sweep_deg": 35.6322, "plate_length_scale": 1.067, "plate_width_scale": 1.0617, "deck_gap": 0.0302, "battery_wedge_deg": 0.0, "plate_thickness_scale": 1.0643, "material": 0.0716} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.44, "cold_headwind": 4.18, "crosswind": 4.03, "gusty_light": 3.86, "hot_thin": 3.68, "storm": 5.03}

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g0 `59b9a52c` fitness 7.127 Wh/km-agg — was: Compact, wide, stiff-armed storm-hardened design with a modest sweep shift and generous deck_gap/plate scale margin to clear the prior rotor-to-deck failure.
- g0 `7792e425` fitness 6.886 Wh/km-agg — was: Long low-disk-loading arms at exact stock sweep with a safely-oversized plate footprint and carbon construction, targeting hot_thin efficiency without touching the plate-web failure zone.
- g0 `89ffb906` fitness 8.479 Wh/km-agg — was: Storm-hardened stiff bruiser: wide thick arms, long wheelbase and thick carbon plates trade mass for structural robustness, targeting the storm scenario that dominates worst-case fitness.
- g0 `9be55419` fitness 7.598 Wh/km-agg — was: Heavy-duty ASA build with thick plates and wide arms near stock sweep for maximum stiffness/durability robustness in storm gusts, accepting a mass penalty.
- g0 `9f8cb660` fitness 6.908 Wh/km-agg — was: Narrow low-drag arms with a tilted battery to cut frontal area for cold_headwind/crosswind, keeping sweep change modest and deck_gap raised for safety margin.
- g3 `4ac62300` fitness 7.026 Wh/km-agg — was: Near-stock sweep (small deltas, clear of the tongue-collision minimum) with plate scales pushed well above the 0.95 web-crush threshold and a thicker printed PLA+ build to test a tougher, mass-tolerant alternative to carbon.
- g3 `a76a6566` fitness 6.705 Wh/km-agg — was: Wider front/rear sweep for a broader stance to help crosswind/storm stability, paired with generously enlarged (not merely stock) plate scales and deck_gap to safely clear the tongue-bolt and web-crush failures seen at extreme sweep.
- g3 `edbeb491` fitness 6.977 Wh/km-agg — was: Moderate-stiffness storm specialist: wider/thicker arms with less waist-narrowing and tough PA12-CF material plus generous deck_gap, betting that structural robustness (not raw mass-adding bulk like earlier failed 'storm-hardened' attempts) trims the worst-case storm penalty.

RECENT INVALID-DESIGN REASONS (histogram): {"plate web too thin (plate_main 0.32 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "deck gap too small for FC/ESC stack": 3, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.66 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.06 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm tongue bolts miss the main plate": 7, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.48 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.59 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "rotor too close to deck/battery": 3, "arm resonance with rotor 1P": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_top 1.25 mm, stock 1.75 mm)": 1, "rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)": 4, "plate web too thin (plate_mid 1.11 mm, stock 1.47 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.84 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm root tongues collide on the main plate": 5, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.78 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pet_cf (1.5 < 1.6 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.85 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.97 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.4 < 1.6 mm)": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_main 0.73 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "arm tip deflection (pa12_cf)": 1, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.44 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.5 < 1.6 mm)": 2, "plate web too thin (plate_main 1.37 mm, stock 1.90 mm)": 1, "plates too thin for pa12_cf (1.6 < 1.6 mm)": 1}


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

Propose exactly 3 NEW genome vectors that are meaningfully DIFFERENT from the elites and from each other — design hypotheses, not small perturbations. Build on what the trajectory and your earlier proposals' outcomes show worked; avoid repeating what failed. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 7b8317ed — Storm-stiffness specialist: a moderately wider stance (5 deg beyond stock both sweeps, plate scales raised per the sweep-deviation rule) with less waist-narrowing and thicker arms/plates than the elites for gust stiffness, while staying on lightweight carbon to avoid the mass penalty that sank earlier 'storm bruiser' attempts.flew at 7.262 Wh/km
  • candidate ed5a193d — Storm-hardened stiff-arm design with arm_length_scale raised to 1.20 (vs the failed 0.88) to restore rotor tip clearance at a near-stock sweep, plus generous plate margins to guard the tongue-bolt/web-crush zones.flew at 7.377 Wh/km
  • candidate 0744daaf — Wide-stance crosswind/storm specialist that pushes both sweeps ~6-7 deg past stock for a broader footprint, with plate_length/width_scale raised well past 1.0 (per the ~0.1-per-5-deg rule) to keep the tongue bolts seated and the plate webs thick.flew at 6.943 Wh/km
  • candidate 9a762f4b — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Compact minimal-mass frame: shortens the wheelbase and thins arm/plate stock well below the elite cluster to shed frame mass for hot_thin/cruise efficiency, betting that the elites' long-arm strategy has more mass to give up than disk-loading penalty to pay at stock sweep.
  • candidate 8e3389f7 — rejected pre-flight (plate web too thin (plate_main 1.44 mm, stock 1.90 mm)) — Low-drag headwind/crosswind specialist: minimum arm width and waist plus an aggressive near-max battery tilt at exactly stock sweep to cut frontal/cross-sectional area, targeting the wind-facing scenarios rather than mass.
